---
title: "Tech Employment in the Age of AI: Milestone 2 EDA"
subtitle: "We Came, We Saw, We Modelled"
author: "Jensen Harvey (group leader), Kaitlyn Chou, and Emily Friedman"
date: "DS 4002 | `r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
    df_print: paged
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
                      fig.width = 9, fig.height = 5, fig.align = "center")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1. Setup and Data Loading

```{r load-libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(scales)
library(forecast)
library(tseries)
library(zoo)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(corrplot)
```

```{r load-data}

df <- read_csv("/Users/jensen.harvey/Desktop/data science/project2/Tech_layoffs.csv")

glimpse(df)
head(df)
```

```{r data-cleaning}
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    YearMonth = floor_date(Date_layoffs, "month"),
    Month = month(Date_layoffs, label = TRUE, abbr = TRUE),
    Laid_Off = as.numeric(Laid_Off),
    Percentage = as.numeric(Percentage)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Date_layoffs), !is.na(Laid_Off))

cat("Date range:", as.character(min(df$Date_layoffs)), "to", as.character(max(df$Date_layoffs)), "\n")
cat("Total records:", nrow(df), "\n")
cat("Total employees laid off:", comma(sum(df$Laid_Off, na.rm = TRUE)), "\n")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2. Total Tech Layoffs Over Time (Monthly Aggregation)

```{r layoffs-over-time}
monthly_layoffs <- df %>%
  group_by(YearMonth) %>%
  summarise(Total_Laid_Off = sum(Laid_Off, na.rm = TRUE),
            N_Companies = n_distinct(Company),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(YearMonth)

ggplot(monthly_layoffs, aes(x = YearMonth, y = Total_Laid_Off)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#1B7A8C", alpha = 0.80) +
  geom_line(color = "#d7191c", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = "#0D2B45", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7,
              fill = "#A8D8E0", alpha = 0.25) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "6 months", date_labels = "%b %Y") +
  labs(
    title = "Monthly Tech Layoffs (2020–Present)",
    subtitle = "Bar = monthly total; Red line = raw trend; Dashed = LOESS smoother",
    x = "Month", y = "Employees Laid Off"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

```{r layoffs-annual}
annual_layoffs <- df %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  summarise(Total_Laid_Off = sum(Laid_Off, na.rm = TRUE),
            N_Companies = n_distinct(Company),
            .groups = "drop")

ggplot(annual_layoffs, aes(x = factor(Year), y = Total_Laid_Off, fill = factor(Year))) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, alpha = 0.88) +
  geom_text(aes(label = comma(Total_Laid_Off)), vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "2020" = "#A8D8E0",
    "2021" = "#6FC4D0",
    "2022" = "#2AACBE",
    "2023" = "#1B7A8C",
    "2024" = "#135E6E",
    "2025" = "#0D2B45"
  )) +
  labs(
    title = "Annual Tech Layoffs by Year",
    x = "Year", y = "Total Employees Laid Off"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3. Time Series Decomposition

```{r ts-decomposition}
start_year  <- year(min(monthly_layoffs$YearMonth))
start_month <- month(min(monthly_layoffs$YearMonth))

layoffs_ts <- ts(monthly_layoffs$Total_Laid_Off,
                 start = c(start_year, start_month),
                 frequency = 12)

layoffs_stl <- stl(layoffs_ts, s.window = "periodic", robust = TRUE)
autoplot(layoffs_stl) +
  labs(title = "STL Decomposition of Monthly Tech Layoffs",
       subtitle = "Trend, Seasonal, and Remainder components") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 4. Stationarity Testing (ADF Test) and Differencing

```{r stationarity}
adf_raw <- adf.test(layoffs_ts, alternative = "stationary")
cat("ADF Test — Original Series\n")
cat("Test Statistic:", round(adf_raw$statistic, 3), "\n")
cat("p-value:", round(adf_raw$p.value, 4), "\n")
cat("Conclusion:", ifelse(adf_raw$p.value < 0.05, "Stationary", "Non-stationary (differencing needed)"), "\n\n")

layoffs_diff <- diff(layoffs_ts, differences = 1)
adf_diff <- adf.test(layoffs_diff, alternative = "stationary")
cat("ADF Test — After First Differencing\n")
cat("Test Statistic:", round(adf_diff$statistic, 3), "\n")
cat("p-value:", round(adf_diff$p.value, 4), "\n")
cat("Conclusion:", ifelse(adf_diff$p.value < 0.05, "Stationary", "Still non-stationary"), "\n")
```

```{r plot-differencing}
par(mfrow = c(2,1), mar = c(4,4,2,1))
plot(layoffs_ts, main = "Original Series: Monthly Layoffs", ylab = "Layoffs", col = "#2c7bb6", lwd = 1.5)
plot(layoffs_diff, main = "First-Differenced Series", ylab = "Differencing Layoffs", col = "#d7191c", lwd = 1.5)
par(mfrow = c(1,1))
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 5. ACF and PACF Plots

```{r acf-pacf}
par(mfrow = c(2,2), mar = c(4,4,3,1))

acf(layoffs_ts,  lag.max = 36, main = "ACF — Original Series")
pacf(layoffs_ts, lag.max = 36, main = "PACF — Original Series")

acf(layoffs_diff,  lag.max = 36, main = "ACF — Differenced Series")
pacf(layoffs_diff, lag.max = 36, main = "PACF — Differenced Series")

par(mfrow = c(1,1))
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 6. Distribution of Layoff Events (Company-Level)

```{r layoff-distribution}
p1 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Laid_Off)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 60, fill = "#4575b4", color = "white", alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_x_log10(labels = comma) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Layoffs per Event (log scale)",
       x = "Employees Laid Off (log)", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

p2 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Laid_Off)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "#74add1", outlier.color = "#d7191c", outlier.alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_x_log10(labels = comma) +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of Layoff Size per Event (log scale)",
       x = "Employees Laid Off (log)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

p1 / p2
```

```{r top-events}
df %>%
  arrange(desc(Laid_Off)) %>%
  select(Company, Date_layoffs, Laid_Off, Industry, Stage) %>%
  head(15) %>%
  rename(`Laid Off` = Laid_Off, Date = Date_layoffs) %>%
  knitr::kable(caption = "Top 15 Largest Single Layoff Events")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 7. Layoffs by Industry

```{r industry-layoffs}
industry_summary <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Industry)) %>%
  group_by(Industry) %>%
  summarise(Total_Laid_Off = sum(Laid_Off, na.rm = TRUE),
            N_Events = n(),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Laid_Off)) %>%
  head(15)

ggplot(industry_summary, aes(x = reorder(Industry, Total_Laid_Off), y = Total_Laid_Off, fill = Total_Laid_Off)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, alpha = 0.9) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#abd9e9", high = "#2c7bb6") +
  labs(
    title = "Total Layoffs by Industry (Top 15)",
    x = "Industry", y = "Total Employees Laid Off"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

```{r industry-over-time}
top_industries <- industry_summary$Industry[1:6]

df %>%
  filter(Industry %in% top_industries) %>%
  group_by(YearMonth, Industry) %>%
  summarise(Total_Laid_Off = sum(Laid_Off, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = YearMonth, y = Total_Laid_Off, color = Industry)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "6 months", date_labels = "%b %Y") +
  labs(
    title = "Monthly Layoffs Over Time — Top 6 Industries",
    x = "Month", y = "Employees Laid Off", color = "Industry"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "bottom")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 8. Seasonality Analysis

```{r seasonality-boxplot}
df %>%
  group_by(Year, Month) %>%
  summarise(Total_Laid_Off = sum(Laid_Off, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Month, y = Total_Laid_Off, fill = Month)) +
  geom_boxplot(show.legend = FALSE, alpha = 0.8, outlier.color = "#d7191c") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Monthly Layoffs by Calendar Month",
    subtitle = "Boxplot across all years — reveals seasonal patterns",
    x = "Month", y = "Total Layoffs"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

```{r seasonal-heatmap}
monthly_layoffs %>%
  mutate(Year = year(YearMonth), Month = month(YearMonth, label = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Month, y = factor(Year), fill = Total_Laid_Off)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#EAF4F7", high = "#0D2B45", labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Heatmap of Monthly Layoffs by Year",
    subtitle = "Darker = more layoffs",
    x = "Month", y = "Year", fill = "Layoffs"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 9. Geographic Distribution of Layoffs

```{r geo-country}
df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Country)) %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%
  summarise(Total_Laid_Off = sum(Laid_Off, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Laid_Off)) %>%
  head(12) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Country, Total_Laid_Off), y = Total_Laid_Off, fill = Total_Laid_Off)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, alpha = 0.9) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#abd9e9", high = "#2c7bb6") +
  labs(title = "Total Layoffs by Country (Top 12)",
       x = "Country", y = "Employees Laid Off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

```{r geo-usstate}
df %>%
  filter(Country == "USA", !is.na(USState)) %>%
  group_by(USState) %>%
  summarise(Total_Laid_Off = sum(Laid_Off, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Laid_Off)) %>%
  head(12) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(USState, Total_Laid_Off), y = Total_Laid_Off, fill = Total_Laid_Off)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, alpha = 0.9) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#abd9e9", high = "#2c7bb6") +
  labs(title = "Total US Layoffs by State (Top 12)",
       x = "State", y = "Employees Laid Off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 10. Layoffs by Company Stage

```{r stage-analysis}
df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Stage)) %>%
  group_by(Stage) %>%
  summarise(Total_Laid_Off = sum(Laid_Off, na.rm = TRUE),
            Median_Pct = median(Percentage, na.rm = TRUE),
            N_Events = n(),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Laid_Off)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Stage, Total_Laid_Off), y = Total_Laid_Off, fill = Median_Pct)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.9) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#e0f3f8", high = "#d73027", name = "Median\nLayoff %") +
  labs(
    title = "Layoffs by Company Stage",
    subtitle = "Fill = median percentage of workforce laid off",
    x = "Stage", y = "Total Employees Laid Off"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 11. Percentage of Workforce Laid Off Over Time

```{r pct-over-time}
df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Percentage)) %>%
  group_by(YearMonth) %>%
  summarise(
    Avg_Pct = mean(Percentage, na.rm = TRUE),
    Med_Pct = median(Percentage, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Avg_Pct, Med_Pct), names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = YearMonth, y = Value, color = Metric)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7) +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "6 months", date_labels = "%b %Y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Avg_Pct" = "#d7191c", "Med_Pct" = "#2c7bb6"),
                     labels = c("Mean %", "Median %")) +
  labs(
    title = "Average & Median % of Workforce Laid Off per Month",
    x = "Month", y = "% of Workforce", color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "bottom")
```
